comment.body: Taking a shot with my strat, JPM collar creates the support at 5565, it stays as support as long as vol comes down.  But as we move up dealers go into shorter vanna do to the ITM puts that close out by the end of the week which will create a resistance ( I think thats why the charm effect is also low because they are ITM). What we want to see is the left tail of the skew come down and the right tail go up on Monday. This would show that puts are being sold and removing that upside vanna. Now if that doesn't happen we're just going to hover around the JPM strike until OPEX

comment.body:
Calm as a cucumber, but frustrated 🥒 

1) I don’t think giving context to your “tactic” by saying you are holding on to an ambiguous longer bought put while buying longer dated calls for your OPEX bounce would be “giving out positions”. What I said as an example of what you could’ve said would be very ambiguous. And again, why share the “tactic” in the first place if you are implementing a different tactic?
2/3) so what has changed today where now a squeeze is creating liquidity and low volatility as compared to yesterday? I would consider 120 point rally in 3 hours a “rip”. Shouldn’t vol be up right now?
4) I quoted what you said. You said, “without any directional bias”. I read you manage a bigger portfolio. But if the positions in that portfolio affect how you trade the rest of the market, then I would consider that part of your “tactic”. Especially when sharing these positions once they are green on the trading signals chat to prove you are being profitable. I don’t see what portfolio they are in matters in this context?
5) was it really that obvious to people here? After those posts yesterday there were a lot of people just sitting on the sidelines. I didn’t see anyone commenting how they are buying calls or shares for your OPEX bounce. Many were wondering if your OPEX bounce call was dead, and many speculating that people holding options into today’s expiration would be screwed. I can share screenshots if you wish.
6) I definitely do not want to copy you. I certainly wouldn’t sell any options yesterday, and if one sold calls they’d be in a world of hurt or stopped out. But I was wondering about your OPEX bounce position and seeming contradictions between your analysis on Wednesday night and Thursday morning.

I actually asked you about this contradiction yesterday after your post, as I mentioned, because a lot of your subscribers were speculating whether your bullish bias into March OPEX was dead. You didn’t respond.

Also, I would assume when you communicate your “tactic” you are referencing your “trading tactics”. So again, what you implemented, regardless of what portfolio it was in, was different than what you communicated.

I’ll just leave this here. I don’t think I’m alone here in saying that your post yesterday certainly added a lot of confusion to your March OPEX bounce call. It certainly did not come off like that was your primary expectation yesterday given what you said about the trading tactic you were implementing, the lack of bullish position taking on this thread, and the speculation amongst subscribers as to what your post meant in regards to your March OPEX rally.

comment.body:
... - asked Sharcus calmly, right?
1) thats a free post, i won't give out my positions in a free post, especially not bcs my portfolio is a bit more complicated than what you see (I signaled reversion trades, not portfolio set ups)

2) "short vol trade" is a jargon saying "betting on vol down", you can do it in your style.
The post's purpose was to sum the infos and descript how the vol effects the current positioning. 

3) Yesterday bcs of the squeeze potential in Mag7, it was so, bcs if squeeze happens and vol spikes that indeed removes liquidity, bcs liqiudity is invers to vol as you know from my educational posts.
And bcs their purpose was to fill the market with liquidity, I said that we gonna see rangebounding, that I'm still calling as the base case. I explained why.

4) I didn't say I DIDN'T have directional take, I said, my TACTIC is to not to be married to anyside until you don't see stabilization at the lvls, and again, I described the environment so you can check it by your own, according to your personal strategy you are doing. 
Again, i say: I manage a bigger portfolio as said above (you clearly doN't read what I say fully, and you miss lines)... i don't post all of my trades, but saying "I"m long biased" is pretty straight-forward... also check my twitter post prior...

5) I didn't said bcs 1) it was obvious for the majority here bcs i keep taling about it for a while, 2) and bcs it was free post. 

6) shorting vol in through any trade it is possible (selling puts, selling calls, short skew, long call/futs betting on vol down/spot up bounce etc.) is very much an idea/tactic. You don't have to copy me, and I don't want that. Read pl what i jsut posted first in the trading signals channel. But I explained how vol can have directional influences in the current environment.

I really ask you to take some more time to study my posts.

comment.body:
ChatGPT said historically not necessarily it will be down: Historically, U.S. government shutdowns have had varied immediate effects on the stock market. Here’s a chronological overview of notable shutdowns and the S&P 500’s performance during those periods:
	1.	November 14–19, 1995:
	•	Duration: 6 days
	•	S&P 500 Performance: Increased by approximately 0.8% over the shutdown period.  ￼
	2.	December 16, 1995 – January 6, 1996:
	•	Duration: 21 days
	•	S&P 500 Performance: Experienced a modest gain of about 0.3% during this period.  ￼
	3.	October 1–16, 2013:
	•	Duration: 16 days
	•	S&P 500 Performance: The index rose approximately 3.1% by the end of the shutdown.  ￼

comment.body:
It sounds to me like you’re intentionally kinda attacking her. A few folks have already parsed and interpreted the question for you. “Supportive” doesn’t automatically mean a rally..it just means the market isn’t gonna drop any further. The market isn’t just a mix of rallies and crashes, ya know? Did you catch what she said? Short vol. If we’re in long vanna, that’s supportive; if we’re in short vanna, then it isn’t. Plus, she mentioned that this effect will fade as we approach OPEX. The market’s main goal is to get liquidity, which means vol down, right? That might trigger a rally, or maybe not.
Also, if you jump into the trading signals channel and check out the weekly intraday threads (which get linked every other day for folks like you who give a damn), you’ll see she kept saying she’s personally betting on a bounce, but that short vol is a sure thing. Check out the very first message from this morning. Also, does it sound familiar that the Arabs scooped up a small portion of tech? Me too..
Really, pay closer attention. How come everyone else gets it? Maybe instead of randomly posting here random stuffs, you should pull up Alma’s posts and ask questions if something isn’t clear, instead of spitting your frustration on everyone

6) I definitely do not want to copy you. I certainly wouldn’t sell any options yesterday, and if one sold calls they’d be in a world of hurt or stopped out. But I was wondering about your OPEX bounce position and seeming contradictions between your analysis on Wednesday night and Thursday morning.

I actually asked you about this contradiction yesterday after your post, as I mentioned, because a lot of your subscribers were speculating whether your bullish bias into March OPEX was dead. You didn’t respond.

Also, I would assume when you communicate your “tactic” you are referencing your “trading tactics”. So again, what you implemented, regardless of what portfolio it was in, was different than what you communicated.

I’ll just leave this here. I don’t think I’m alone here in saying that your post yesterday certainly added a lot of confusion to your March OPEX bounce call. It certainly did not come off like that was your primary expectation yesterday given what you said about the trading tactic you were implementing, the lack of bullish position taking on this thread, and the speculation amongst subscribers as to what your post meant in regards to your March OPEX rally.

comment.body:
Calm as a cucumber, but frustrated 🥒 

1) I don’t think giving context to your “tactic” by saying you are holding on to an ambiguous longer bought put while buying longer dated calls for your OPEX bounce would be “giving out positions”. What I said as an example of what you could’ve said would be very ambiguous. And again, why share the “tactic” in the first place if you are implementing a different tactic?
2/3) so what has changed today where now a squeeze is creating liquidity and low volatility as compared to yesterday? I would consider 120 point rally in 3 hours a “rip”. Shouldn’t vol be up right now?
4) I quoted what you said. You said, “without any directional bias”. I read you manage a bigger portfolio. But if the positions in that portfolio affect how you trade the rest of the market, then I would consider that part of your “tactic”. Especially when sharing these positions once they are green on the trading signals chat to prove you are being profitable. I don’t see what portfolio they are in matters in this context?

I didn’t see anyone commenting how they are buying calls or shares for your OPEX bounce. comment.body: Actually, I see it differently.  It's not an attack, sounds like clarification to me.  If you're asking people to pay for your thoughts and convictions, then the buyer of that service has the right to ask for clarification.  I have pay for other subs and nobody is this defensive when they ask for the same insight.  It's a fair question, imo.

comment.body:
It sounds to me like you’re intentionally kinda attacking her. A few folks have already parsed and interpreted the question for you. “Supportive” doesn’t automatically mean a rally..it just means the market isn’t gonna drop any further. The market isn’t just a mix of rallies and crashes, ya know? Did you catch what she said? Short vol. If we’re in long vanna, that’s supportive; if we’re in short vanna, then it isn’t. Plus, she mentioned that this effect will fade as we approach OPEX. The market’s main goal is to get liquidity, which means vol down, right? That might trigger a rally, or maybe not.
Also, if you jump into the trading signals channel and check out the weekly intraday threads (which get linked every other day for folks like you who give a damn), you’ll see she kept saying she’s personally betting on a bounce, but that short vol is a sure thing. Check out the very first message from this morning. Also, does it sound familiar that the Arabs scooped up a small portion of tech? Me too..
Really, pay closer attention. How come everyone else gets it? Maybe instead of randomly posting here random stuffs, you should pull up Alma’s posts and ask questions if something isn’t clear, instead of spitting your frustration on everyone

comment.body:
It’s still down by 8% today. And volatility isn’t up as Alma mentioned would happen yesterday. It’s just less down than what it would be. And 120 rip is 1.69% higher than where we closed yesterday. From a directional perspective, that’s a large rip.

Also, if we were shorting vol, then I guess you’d be up 8% today… or more.
So that’s good if you shorted puts. But why would you short puts if the whole narrative of that vol decay yesterday was attributed to the price of SPX staying below the JPM put strike at 5565. We are now trading well above it and held that support this morning.

So question for the thread, who made money yesterday shorting volatility, as Alma suggested?

Just an honest question. If there was a trade where you shorted vol as Alma suggested and you made money, would love to see it, even for educational purposes and to further cement the direct implementation of her analysis.

So that’s good if you shorted puts. But why would you short puts if the whole narrative of that vol decay yesterday was attributed to the price of SPX staying below the JPM put strike at 5565. We are now trading well above it and held that support this morning.

So question for the thread, who made money yesterday shorting volatility, as Alma suggested?

Just an honest question. If there was a trade where you shorted vol as Alma suggested and you made money, would love to see it, even for educational purposes and to further cement the direct implementation of her analysis.

comment.body: 120 pt rally in 3 hours with a vix above 21 is not a rip. Thats just staying within the implied range which is why you can't have a directional bias. realized volatility isn't catching up which is why the short vol trade is the play. You will get burned going any direction
